typeMetadata.abstract: E.L.I.A. associates every data element with a 64‑bit Type Metadata envelope  that enables deterministic parsing, validation, and serialization...

seq.H3: Relationship to Data and Informational Context

typeSerialization.abstract: E.L.I.A. associates every data element with a 64‑bit Type Metadata envelope  that enables deterministic parsing, validation, and serialization...

securityContext.abstract: E.L.I.A. associates every data element with a 64‑bit Type Metadata envelope  that enables deterministic parsing, validation, and serialization...

datatypes.abstract: The E.L.I.A. language employs an extended orthogonal type classification model, based not on a single criterion but on a set of independent dimensions.

primitiveTypesRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

seq.H2: Block ⇄ Stream Promotion and Demotion Rules (Normative)

blockTypesRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

derivedTypesRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

seq.H2: Stream ⇄ Block Promotion and Demotion Rules (Normative)

streamTypesRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

semanticTypesRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

non-value.abstract: Special/control primitives represent semantic control markers, not domain data.

primitive_types.abstract: Primitive types are used to encode primitive information — irreducible factual values that have no internal structure at the language level. Includes GUID/RIID identifier encoding, literal formats, and the complete type compatibility/conversion matrix.

integral_type.abstract: The Integral class defines signed and unsigned integer types (int8–int64, uint8–uint64) with fixed bit widths, two's complement encoding, and explicit overflow semantics.

decimal_floating_type.abstract: The Decimal/floating class defines float, double, and decimal types with IEEE 754 encoding, precision guarantees, and normative rounding rules.

boolean_type.abstract: The Boolean class defines a single bool type with normative binary encoding (0x00=false, 0x01=true) and constraints prohibiting any other byte values.

datetime_type.abstract: The Date/Time class defines temporal primitive types (date, time, datetime, timestamp, timespan) with normative encoding rules and calendar-independent representation.

enum_type.abstract: Enum defines finite named value sets with explicit ordinal semantics, explicit initialization requirements, and compile-time validation of exhaustiveness.

fixed_string_type.abstract: Fixed String is a primitive type with compile-time capacity constraint — always serializes to exactly N bytes with no length prefix, used for protocol-level fixed-width fields.

type_conversion_rules.abstract: Normative type conversion rules: implicit assignment (widening only), explicit conversion matrices for numeric and non-numeric primitives, special/control type rules, and the fundamental principle of nominal typing.

primitive_encoding_rules.abstract: Binary encoding rules for all primitive types: canonical principles, 48-bit inline threshold, endianness requirements, parser obligations with validation algorithm, and performance optimization guidelines for inline vs external primitives.

block_types.abstract: Block types are used to model opaque or structurally fixed or dynamic data payloads whose internal representation is either intentionally hidden...

opaque_block_types.abstract: Opaque block types represent indivisible, immutable binary payloads with explicitly declared or limited with 26-bits size semantics.

seq.H1: Binary Encoding Rules for Block Types

structured_block.abstract: Structural blocks mainly represent derived types with schema-defined, immutable data compositions built from primitives and block values.

derived_types.abstract: Derived types define enveloped semantic specializations that apply only to data-carrying and declarative constructs.

specialized_types.abstract: Specialized Types are fundamental data constructs whose semantics are rigidly defined by the language itself.

stream_types.abstract: Stream types represent sequential, potentially unbounded data carriers designed for controlled input/output, transport, and incremental...

stream_carrier_types.abstract: Stream carriers represent potentially unbounded, sequential data exchange formats intended for transport of large payloads, continuous data, or...

stream_structural_types.abstract: Structural stream mainly represent derived types with schema-defined, immutable data compositions built from primitives and block values.

stream_raw_string_types.abstract: RAW String represents a specialized stream type for large or unbounded textual data that must be processed and transported as a stream rather...

stream_raw_binary_types.abstract: RAW Binary represents large or streaming binary data that cannot be safely or efficiently represented as bounded opaque blocks.

error_type.abstract: A specialized derived block-materialized data type representing an immutable failure or violation fact.

event_type.abstract: a specialized derived block-materialized data type representing an immutable factual occurrence.

array_type.abstract: A Collection is a semantic derived type representing an iterable container abstraction conceptually based on a one-dimensional array (rank = 1).

derived_array_type.abstract: A derived array is a named, first-class array type declared at the domain level.

collection_type.abstract: A Collection is a semantic derived type representing an iterable container abstraction conceptually based on a one-dimensional array (rank = 1).

record_type.abstract: A record  represents a structured block-materialized, schema-defined derived value composed of named fields.

dictionary_type.abstract: A dictionary represents a key–value derived container with explicitly typed keys and values, intended for structured associative data.

any_type.abstract: any is a special polymorphic primitive or block-materialized data container that guarantees preservation of the physical properties of the...

tuple_type.abstract: tuple is a flexible data composition construct that combines multiple values into a single structured block.

version_type.abstract: version is an immutable factual record composed of four ordered components — major, minor, build, and revision — used to express...

reference_type.abstract: reference is a specialized embedded data type used to form explicit, canonical references to objects beyond the current application or domain boundary.

expTree_type.abstract: The exptree is a specialized block-materialized data type that encodes permission derivation, validation, and enforcement logic strictly as data. Connected to the infer construct for rule-based evaluation.

behavioral_types.abstract: Behavioral types represent active semantic constructs in E.L.I.A. They embody *executable intent, authority, and responsibility* and are...

capable_types.abstract: Capable types represent where behavior actually runs. They own execution context, may hold state, invoke algorithms, and apply transformations.

orchestration_type.abstract: Purpose: composition and coordination — definition of multi-step execution topology.

authority_types.abstract: Authority types define semantic permission, obligation, and contractual boundaries.

class_type.abstract: A Class is a *capable behavioral construct* and derived type that represents a concrete, state-bearing execution unit.

inline_type.abstract: An Inline construct is a *capable behavioral construct* that is materialized as a behavior surrogate and provides a localized, embedded...

binder.abstract: A Binder is a *capable behavioral construct* that defines a deterministic transformation contract: it maps an input value or structure into...

interface_type.abstract: An Interface is an *authority behavioral construct* that defines a declarative contract of capability and responsibility without providing...

rule_type.abstract: A Rule is an *authority behavioral construct* that defines deterministic semantic reasoning logic. Rules are classified into normative classes R0–R6, each with strict constraints on inputs, evaluation stage, and enforcement participation.

action_type.abstract: An Action is an *authority behavioral construct* that declares a business-meaningful intent which may be executed as part of a flow once admitted.

flow_type.abstract: A Flow is an *orchestration behavioral construct* that defines a transactional, multi-step execution topology.

method_type.abstract: A Method is a *capable behavioral construct* that represents a named, callable unit of domain and module-internal logic. It executes computation, transformation, validation, or queries — an imperative execution substrate with full control-flow capability.
